,"name":"test_saleQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_saleQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOutput","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_knownSmallInput_expectedRange","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_legacyDenom_overflow_reverts","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_safeBoundary_succeeds","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_sumOverflow_reverts","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term1Overflow_reverts","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term2Overflow_reverts","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"details":"Created as part of CRANE-026 to strengthen overflow boundary testing. This file tests two primary overflow-prone functions: 1. `_swapDepositSaleAmt` - Quadratic path with multi-multiply expressions:    - term1 = twoMinusFee * twoMinusFee * saleReserve * saleReserve    - term2 = 4 * oneMinusFee * feeDenominator * amountIn * saleReserve 2. `_calculateFeePortionForPosition` - Multi-multiply expressions:    - sA = (initialA * initialB * reserveA) / reserveB    - sB = (initialA * initialB * reserveB) / reserveA Key principle: Solidity 0.8+ uses checked arithmetic by default, so overflow should revert rather than silently wrap. These tests prove that behavior. ## Overflow Magnitude Analysis For `_swapDepositSaleAmt`: - With FEE_DENOMINATOR = 100000, feePercent = 300:   - twoMinusFee = 2*100000 - 300 = 199700 \u2248 2e5   - twoMinusFee\u00b2 \u2248 4e10   - term1 = 4e10 * saleReserve\u00b2 \u2192 overflow when saleReserve > ~1.07e37   - term2 = 4e10 * amountIn * saleReserve \u2192 overflow when amountIn * saleReserve > ~2.9e66 For `_calculateFeePortionForPosition`: - sA = initialA * initialB * reserveA / reserveB - Overflow when initialA * initialB * reserveA > type(uint256).max \u2248 1.16e77 - With all values ~1e26, product ~1e78 which exceeds uint256.max ## Summary of Overflow-Triggering Magnitudes | Function                        | Parameter     | Safe Upper Bound | Overflow Trigger | |---------------------------------|---------------|------------------|------------------| | _swapDepositSaleAmt             | saleReserve   | ~1.7e33         | ~1e34+           | | _swapDepositSaleAmt             | amountIn*res  | ~2.9e66 product | product > 2.9e66 | | _calculateFeePortionForPosition | initialA/B    | ~1e25 each      | ~1e26+ all       | | _calculateFeePortionForPosition | reserveA/B    | ~1e25 each      | ~1e26+ all       | | _calculateFeePortionForPosition | claimable     | no limit*       | uses _mulDiv     | *Note: claimable calculation uses Math._mulDiv with 512-bit intermediate precision,        so it does not overflow even with extreme ownedLP * reserveA values.","kind":"dev","methods":{"testFuzz_calculateFeePortionForPosition_extremeInitials_reverts(uint256,uint256)":{"details":"Values where initialA * initialB * max(reserveA, reserveB) > uint256.max"},"testFuzz_swapDepositSaleAmt_extremeReserve_reverts(uint256)":{"details":"Values above 1e38 should consistently trigger overflow."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_asymmetricOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"One very large initial with moderately large others."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_claimableSafeWithMulDiv()":{"details":"The function uses Math._mulDiv which safely handles ownedLP * reserveA / totalSupply      even when ownedLP * reserveA would overflow. This test proves the library's      overflow-safe multiplication/division works correctly. Math breakdown: - claimableA = _mulDiv(ownedLP, reserveA, totalSupply) = _mulDiv(1e50, 1e50, 1e51) = 1e49 - claimableB = _mulDiv(ownedLP, reserveB, totalSupply) = _mulDiv(1e50, 1e18, 1e51) = 1e17 - noFeeA = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveA / reserveB) = sqrt(1e10 * 1e10 * 1e50 / 1e18)          = sqrt(1e52) \u2248 3.16e25 - noFeeB = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveB / reserveA) = sqrt(1e10 * 1e10 * 1e18 / 1e50)          = sqrt(1e-12) = 0 (integer sqrt truncation) Since claimableA (1e49) >> noFeeA (3.16e25), feeA = claimableA - noFeeA \u2248 1e49 Since noFeeB = 0 and claimableB = 1e17, feeB = claimableB = 1e17"},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_poolGrowth_positiveFees()":{"details":"When reserves have grown (claimable > initial), fees should be positive."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sAOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"sA = (initialA * initialB * reserveA) / reserveB overflows before division Calculation for overflow boundary: - For overflow: initialA * initialB * reserveA > 2^256 \u2248 1.16e77 - With all values equal: value\u00b3 > 1.16e77 \u2192 value > 4.88e25 Using initialA = initialB = reserveA = 1e26, product = 1e78 > uint256.max"},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sBOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"sB = (initialA * initialB * reserveB) / reserveA overflows before division Using large reserveB and small reserveA to trigger sB path overflow."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_safeBoundary_succeeds()":{"details":"initialA * initialB * reserve < uint256.max should succeed. For symmetric inputs (initialA == initialB == reserveA == reserveB): - claimable = ownedLP * reserve / totalSupply - noFee = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveA / reserveB) = sqrt(initialA * initialB) = initial - fee = claimable - noFee (clamped to 0) When claimable > initial, we have fee > 0 (pool grew from fees). When claimable <= initial, fee = 0 (no fee growth or IL)."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_totalFeeBound()":{"details":"This is a critical invariant: total fees cannot exceed total value claimable."},"test_purchaseQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"_purchaseQuote has (reserveIn * amountOut) * feeDenominator"},"test_purchaseQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOutput()":{"details":"The amount required to purchase a given output should be:      - Greater than amountOut (fees and slippage)      - Less than reserveIn for small outputs"},"test_saleQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"_saleQuote has amountIn * (feeDenominator - feePercent) * reserveOut For overflow: amountIn * ~1e5 * reserveOut > 2^256 With amountIn = reserveOut = 1e38, product \u2248 1e81 > uint256.max"},"test_saleQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOutput()":{"details":"With balanced reserves and moderate swap, output should be:      - Less than reserveOut (can't drain pool)      - Less than amountIn (fee reduces output)      - Proportional to the swap math"},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_knownSmallInput_expectedRange()":{"details":"For a deposit of X tokens with large reserves, the optimal swap amount      should approach X/2 (half swapped to balance the deposit)."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_legacyDenom_overflow_reverts()":{"details":"Legacy pools use smaller denominator, affecting overflow boundaries. With feeDenominator = 1000, feePercent = 3 (0.3%): - twoMinusFee = 2*1000 - 3 = 1997 \u2248 2e3 - twoMinusFee\u00b2 \u2248 4e6 - term1 = 4e6 * saleReserve\u00b2 \u2192 overflow when saleReserve > ~1.7e35"},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_safeBoundary_succeeds()":{"details":"term1 = twoMinusFee\u00b2 * saleReserve\u00b2 = (2e5)\u00b2 * saleReserve\u00b2 = 4e10 * saleReserve\u00b2      For no overflow: 4e10 * saleReserve\u00b2 < 2^256 \u2248 1.16e77      Therefore: saleReserve\u00b2 < 2.9e66 \u2192 saleReserve < 1.7e33      Using saleReserve = 1e32 provides comfortable safety margin."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_sumOverflow_reverts()":{"details":"Even if individual terms don't overflow, their sum might. Using values that are close to but below individual overflow thresholds, but whose sum exceeds uint256.max."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term1Overflow_reverts()":{"details":"term1 = twoMinusFee\u00b2 * saleReserve\u00b2 overflows when saleReserve > ~1e37 Calculation for overflow boundary: - twoMinusFee = 2 * 100000 - 300 = 199700 \u2248 2e5 - twoMinusFee\u00b2 \u2248 3.988e10 \u2248 4e10 - For term1 overflow: 4e10 * saleReserve\u00b2 > 2^256 \u2248 1.16e77 - Therefore: saleReserve\u00b2 > 2.9e66 \u2192 saleReserve > 1.7e33 - But with full precision: saleReserve > ~1.07e37 for actual overflow Using saleReserve = 1e39 (above overflow threshold) with moderate amountIn."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term2Overflow_reverts()":{"details":"term2 = 4 * oneMinusFee * feeDenominator * amountIn * saleReserve Calculation for overflow boundary: - oneMinusFee = 100000 - 300 = 99700 \u2248 1e5 - 4 * oneMinusFee * feeDenominator \u2248 4 * 1e5 * 1e5 = 4e10 - For term2 overflow: 4e10 * amountIn * saleReserve > 2^256 \u2248 1.16e77 - Therefore: amountIn * saleReserve > 2.9e66 Using both amountIn and saleReserve = 1e35 \u2192 product = 1e70 > 2.9e66"}},"title":"ConstProdUtils_OverflowBoundary_Test","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"externalCalculateFeePortionForPosition(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"External wrapper for _calculateFeePortionForPosition."},"externalPurchaseQuote(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"External wrapper for _purchaseQuote."},"externalSaleQuote(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"External wrapper for _saleQuote."},"externalSwapDepositSaleAmt(uint256,uint256,uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"External wrapper for _swapDepositSaleAmt to enable try/catch and expectRevert."},"testFuzz_calculateFeePortionForPosition_extremeInitials_reverts(uint256,uint256)":{"notice":"Fuzz test proving overflow reverts for extreme initial values."},"testFuzz_swapDepositSaleAmt_extremeReserve_reverts(uint256)":{"notice":"Fuzz test proving overflow reverts for extreme saleReserve values."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_asymmetricOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests overflow with asymmetric initial values."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_claimableSafeWithMulDiv()":{"notice":"Verifies that _mulDiv safely handles claimable calculation with large values."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_poolGrowth_positiveFees()":{"notice":"Verifies _calculateFeePortionForPosition with pool growth scenario."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sAOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _calculateFeePortionForPosition reverts on sA overflow."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sBOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _calculateFeePortionForPosition reverts on sB overflow."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_safeBoundary_succeeds()":{"notice":"Verifies safe boundary: values just below overflow threshold work."},"test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_totalFeeBound()":{"notice":"Verifies fee + feeB is bounded by total claimable across both tokens."},"test_purchaseQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _purchaseQuote overflow paths revert properly."},"test_purchaseQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOutput()":{"notice":"Verifies _purchaseQuote returns bounded, meaningful results within safe parameters."},"test_saleQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _saleQuote overflow paths also revert properly."},"test_saleQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOutput()":{"notice":"Verifies _saleQuote returns bounded, meaningful results within safe parameters."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_knownSmallInput_expectedRange()":{"notice":"Verifies _swapDepositSaleAmt with known small values produces expected range."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_legacyDenom_overflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests overflow with legacy fee denominator (1000)."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_safeBoundary_succeeds()":{"notice":"Verifies safe boundary: values just below overflow threshold work."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_sumOverflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on combined term1+term2 overflow."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term1Overflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on term1 overflow."},"test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term2Overflow_reverts()":{"notice":"Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on term2 overflow."}},"notice":"Tests proving that overflow conditions revert cleanly (no silent wrapping).","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"test/foundry/spec/utils/math/constProdUtils/ConstProdUtils_OverflowBoundary.t.sol":"ConstProdUtils_OverflowBoundary_Test"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":1},"remappings":[":@crane/contracts/=contracts/",":@crane/test/=test/",":@openzeppelin/contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/contracts/",":erc4626-tests/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/erc4626-tests/",":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/",":halmos-cheatcodes/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/lib/halmos-cheatcodes/src/",":hardhat/=node_modules/hardhat/",":openzeppelin-contracts/=lib/openzeppelin-contracts/",":solady/=lib/solady/src/"]},"sources":{"contracts/GeneralErrors.sol":{"keccak256":"0x89ee7d9a282b2d081416d4d3bd321a19a96c5894ff3b9da7f126c69e3e54a688","license":"AGPL-3.0-or-later","urls":["bzz-raw://11f48ba26e14cc6eb0206df8e3e29483bfd7237867fdf7fb3bd2a32046b14ae3","dweb:/ipfs/QmZ31fpXZYt33v9zf84TTahb5r18niL3TuBYKhVDgQnKTM"]},"contracts/constants/Constants.sol":{"keccak256":"0x24eb01548a74a6f1c6dae77d3ae34bc92d603e7da07532065434b5ae43f88381","license":"AGPL-3.0-or-later","urls":["bzz-raw://c42a51e69f938f8f732d72fd4789bbd707ec38523b6294031fd64549f75ca6f1","dweb:/ipfs/QmNWtZPdxPBEqZrozRkBcbBrFBhkci4sAQycHaVpHyKeuo"]},"contracts/protocols/dexes/camelot/v2/stubs/libraries/Math.sol":{"keccak256":"0x61423c9698a6d28646af00b14669e7a33b3fc405ba04965a8531f7f20ab002ae","license":"AGPL-3.0-or-later","urls":["bzz-raw://289f0906223b5e9affabab9db00e69917875685b650c199cba6f1dfd6c432138","dweb:/ipfs/QmeSq6nq1Q3Fd1J3U5UKiq9JPj5zR8bYUAYAuW4frhJBZ9"]},"contracts/protocols/dexes/uniswap/v2/stubs/deps/libs/Conversion.sol":{"keccak256":"0xdabe2279642fa470bef38eaaba0ae3bba838357504906f9cf2ff28bc8374b247","license":"AGPL-3.0-or-later","urls":["bzz-raw://cb177b08da309e45ec8e1326e693067b7c8e9e914a4ce66927d5c2c638052fa7","dweb:/ipfs/QmTLQa8w9hSyHkhvK5LrJTUQ8nYtchiNjZQG5BqjGo33aG"]},"contracts/protocols/dexes/uniswap/ testFuzz_calculateFeePortionForPosition_.details: Values where initialA * initialB * max(reserveA, reserveB) > uint256.max

testFuzz_swapDepositSaleAmt_extremeReser.details: Values above 1e38 should consistently trigger overflow.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_asym.details: One very large initial with moderately large others.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_clai.details: The function uses Math._mulDiv which safely handles ownedLP * reserveA / totalSupply      even when ownedLP * reserveA would overflow. This test proves the library's      overflow-safe multiplication/division works correctly. Math breakdown: - claimableA = _mulDiv(ownedLP, reserveA, totalSupply) = _mulDiv(1e50, 1e50, 1e51) = 1e49 - claimableB = _mulDiv(ownedLP, reserveB, totalSupply) = _mulDiv(1e50, 1e18, 1e51) = 1e17 - noFeeA = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveA / reserveB) = sqrt(1e10 * 1e10 * 1e50 / 1e18)          = sqrt(1e52) ≈ 3.16e25 - noFeeB = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveB / reserveA) = sqrt(1e10 * 1e10 * 1e18 / 1e50)          = sqrt(1e-12) = 0 (integer sqrt truncation) Since claimableA (1e49) >> noFeeA (3.16e25), feeA = claimableA - noFeeA ≈ 1e49 Since noFeeB = 0 and claimableB = 1e17, feeB = claimableB = 1e17

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_pool.details: When reserves have grown (claimable > initial), fees should be positive.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sAOv.details: sA = (initialA * initialB * reserveA) / reserveB overflows before division Calculation for overflow boundary: - For overflow: initialA * initialB * reserveA > 2^256 ≈ 1.16e77 - With all values equal: value³ > 1.16e77 → value > 4.88e25 Using initialA = initialB = reserveA = 1e26, product = 1e78 > uint256.max

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sBOv.details: sB = (initialA * initialB * reserveB) / reserveA overflows before division Using large reserveB and small reserveA to trigger sB path overflow.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_safe.details: initialA * initialB * reserve < uint256.max should succeed. For symmetric inputs (initialA == initialB == reserveA == reserveB): - claimable = ownedLP * reserve / totalSupply - noFee = sqrt(initialA * initialB * reserveA / reserveB) = sqrt(initialA * initialB) = initial - fee = claimable - noFee (clamped to 0) When claimable > initial, we have fee > 0 (pool grew from fees). When claimable <= initial, fee = 0 (no fee growth or IL).

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_tota.details: This is a critical invariant: total fees cannot exceed total value claimable.

test_purchaseQuote_safeBoundary_meaningf.details: The amount required to purchase a given output should be:      - Greater than amountOut (fees and slippage)      - Less than reserveIn for small outputs

test_saleQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts.details: _saleQuote has amountIn * (feeDenominator - feePercent) * reserveOut For overflow: amountIn * ~1e5 * reserveOut > 2^256 With amountIn = reserveOut = 1e38, product ≈ 1e81 > uint256.max

test_saleQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOu.details: With balanced reserves and moderate swap, output should be:      - Less than reserveOut (can't drain pool)      - Less than amountIn (fee reduces output)      - Proportional to the swap math

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_knownSmallInput_.details: For a deposit of X tokens with large reserves, the optimal swap amount      should approach X/2 (half swapped to balance the deposit).

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_legacyDenom_over.details: Legacy pools use smaller denominator, affecting overflow boundaries. With feeDenominator = 1000, feePercent = 3 (0.3%): - twoMinusFee = 2*1000 - 3 = 1997 ≈ 2e3 - twoMinusFee² ≈ 4e6 - term1 = 4e6 * saleReserve² → overflow when saleReserve > ~1.7e35

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_safeBoundary_suc.details: term1 = twoMinusFee² * saleReserve² = (2e5)² * saleReserve² = 4e10 * saleReserve²      For no overflow: 4e10 * saleReserve² < 2^256 ≈ 1.16e77      Therefore: saleReserve² < 2.9e66 → saleReserve < 1.7e33      Using saleReserve = 1e32 provides comfortable safety margin.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_sumOverflow_reve.details: Even if individual terms don't overflow, their sum might. Using values that are close to but below individual overflow thresholds, but whose sum exceeds uint256.max.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term1Overflow_re.details: term1 = twoMinusFee² * saleReserve² overflows when saleReserve > ~1e37 Calculation for overflow boundary: - twoMinusFee = 2 * 100000 - 300 = 199700 ≈ 2e5 - twoMinusFee² ≈ 3.988e10 ≈ 4e10 - For term1 overflow: 4e10 * saleReserve² > 2^256 ≈ 1.16e77 - Therefore: saleReserve² > 2.9e66 → saleReserve > 1.7e33 - But with full precision: saleReserve > ~1.07e37 for actual overflow Using saleReserve = 1e39 (above overflow threshold) with moderate amountIn.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term2Overflow_re.details: term2 = 4 * oneMinusFee * feeDenominator * amountIn * saleReserve Calculation for overflow boundary: - oneMinusFee = 100000 - 300 = 99700 ≈ 1e5 - 4 * oneMinusFee * feeDenominator ≈ 4 * 1e5 * 1e5 = 4e10 - For term2 overflow: 4e10 * amountIn * saleReserve > 2^256 ≈ 1.16e77 - Therefore: amountIn * saleReserve > 2.9e66 Using both amountIn and saleReserve = 1e35 → product = 1e70 > 2.9e66

externalSwapDepositSaleAmt(uint256,uint2.notice: External wrapper for _swapDepositSaleAmt to enable try/catch and expectRevert.

testFuzz_calculateFeePortionForPosition_.notice: Fuzz test proving overflow reverts for extreme initial values.

testFuzz_swapDepositSaleAmt_extremeReser.notice: Fuzz test proving overflow reverts for extreme saleReserve values.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_asym.notice: Tests overflow with asymmetric initial values.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_clai.notice: Verifies that _mulDiv safely handles claimable calculation with large values.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sAOv.notice: Tests that _calculateFeePortionForPosition reverts on sA overflow.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_sBOv.notice: Tests that _calculateFeePortionForPosition reverts on sB overflow.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_safe.notice: Verifies safe boundary: values just below overflow threshold work.

test_calculateFeePortionForPosition_tota.notice: Verifies fee + feeB is bounded by total claimable across both tokens.

test_purchaseQuote_numeratorOverflow_rev.notice: Tests that _purchaseQuote overflow paths revert properly.

test_purchaseQuote_safeBoundary_meaningf.notice: Verifies _purchaseQuote returns bounded, meaningful results within safe parameters.

test_saleQuote_numeratorOverflow_reverts.notice: Tests that _saleQuote overflow paths also revert properly.

test_saleQuote_safeBoundary_meaningfulOu.notice: Verifies _saleQuote returns bounded, meaningful results within safe parameters.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_knownSmallInput_.notice: Verifies _swapDepositSaleAmt with known small values produces expected range.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_legacyDenom_over.notice: Tests overflow with legacy fee denominator (1000).

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_safeBoundary_suc.notice: Verifies safe boundary: values just below overflow threshold work.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_sumOverflow_reve.notice: Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on combined term1+term2 overflow.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term1Overflow_re.notice: Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on term1 overflow.

test_swapDepositSaleAmt_term2Overflow_re.notice: Tests that _swapDepositSaleAmt reverts on term2 overflow.
